app.name: Zsh-Conf
app.one_liner: A blazing-fast, modular, and aesthetic Zsh configuration framework with ~20ms load times.

screens.name: Installation Wizard
screens.purpose: Guides user through dependency detection, backup creation, and secure setup of the Zsh configuration.
primary_actions: Create timestamped backups of existing configs
Detect operating system and package manager

screens.name: Terminal Shell
screens.purpose: Primary interactive shell interface with enhanced prompt, syntax highlighting, and command execution.
primary_actions: Access lazy-loaded developer tools (Git, Go, Python, Node)

rust_commands.name: benchmark_startup
rust_commands.purpose: Measures and reports shell startup time using the built-in ZSH_BENCHMARK tool.

rust_commands.name: install_framework
rust_commands.purpose: Downloads and sets up the Zsh configuration with environment detection and atomic backups.

rust_commands.name: switch_neovim_profile
rust_commands.purpose: Switches between supported Neovim configurations like LazyVim, NvChad, and AstroNvim.

mvp_plan: week 1: Implement core installer logic with OS detection and backup creation
week 1: Integrate Starship prompt and basic syntax highlighting
week 1: Set up lazy-loading scaffold for plugins
acceptance_tests: After installation, shell startup time is under 25ms on M4 hardware as measured by ZSH_BENCHMARK.
Fancy startup features (ColorScript, sound effects) activate reliably on shell launch.
Installer correctly detects macOS and skips Homebrew dependency installation when not present, showing clear message.
Renaming 'cat' to 'bat' does not break command auto-suggestions.
User-Overrides.sh modifications persist across reinstallation without being overwritten.
